originalTitle: Nigeria to seek compensation for property abandoned by citizens fleeing South Africa
title: Helping Our Friends: Nigeria Wants to Get Back What Its People Left Behind!
content:
Hello, my super friends! It's your kid-friendly reporter bringing you exciting news from our amazing continent, Africa! Today, we're talking about two big and beautiful countries: Nigeria and South Africa. Imagine you have a favorite toy, and you accidentally leave it at your friend's house. You'd want to get it back, right? Well, something a bit like that is happening now, but with grown-up things like houses and shops! Nigeria wants to help its people get back what they left behind in South Africa.

Our African Family: Nigeria and South Africa

Nigeria is a really big country in West Africa, known for its super lively music, yummy jollof rice, and lots of wonderful people! It's like a giant melting pot of cultures and traditions. South Africa is another big country, all the way down at the bottom of Africa, famous for its amazing wildlife like lions and elephants, and beautiful mountains. These two countries are like big brothers in our African family. Did you know? Nigeria has over 200 million people, making it the most populated country in Africa! That's a lot of friends!

Why Did Some People Leave Their Homes?

Many Nigerian people used to live and work in South Africa. They had shops, businesses, and even houses there. They were building their lives, just like your parents go to work and build your home. But sadly, a little while ago, some bad things happened. Some Nigerians felt scared and unsafe in South Africa. It's like when you feel a bit scared at a new place and just want to go home to your family. So, many of them packed their bags and came back home to Nigeria, where they felt safer. Did you know? People who leave one country to go back to their home country are sometimes called 'returnees'! It just means they returned home.

What Did They Leave Behind?

When these Nigerian friends left South Africa quickly, they couldn't take everything with them. Imagine leaving your bedroom, and you can only take one small bag. You'd have to leave your bed, your cupboard, your toys, and maybe even your pet! These grown-ups left behind their shops with all their goods, their offices with their computers, and even their homes with their furniture and special memories. These things are called 'property' or 'businesses.' They are important because they are how people earn money and where they live. Did you know? 'Property' can mean anything you own, from a tiny toy to a huge building! It's all about what belongs to you.

Nigeria Wants to Help Its People

Now, Nigeria wants to help these people who lost their things. Nigeria's special helper, called the 'High Commissioner,' is like a super important ambassador or a 'big boss' for Nigeria in South Africa. This special helper is saying, 'Hey, our friends left their things behind, and it wasn't their fault! Can we please get some help or money for what they lost?' This is called 'compensation.' It means getting something back – like money or help – to make up for something that was lost or broken. Did you know? An 'ambassador' or 'High Commissioner' is like a country's representative. They are there to talk for their country and help their citizens in another country.

Making a Super Important List

The Nigerian High Commissioner is not just wishing for things to happen. They are doing something very important! They are making a big, big list. They are writing down every shop, every house, every business that was left behind by Nigerians. They are checking to see what was there, what was lost, and how much it was all worth. This is called 'documenting.' It's like when your teacher makes a list of all the children in the class and what they need. It helps to keep track of everything and make sure nothing is forgotten. Did you know? Keeping good records and making lists helps grown-ups solve problems fairly! It's like making sure everyone gets a turn in a game.

Why This Is Super Important for Everyone

Why is Nigeria doing all this work? Because it wants to show its people that it cares about them. When someone loses their home or their way of earning money, it can be very sad and difficult. By asking for compensation, Nigeria is trying to help its citizens get back on their feet. It's about fairness and making sure that people who lost things unfairly get some help. It's also about showing that countries can work together to solve problems peacefully and kindly. Did you know? When countries talk to each other to solve problems, it's called 'diplomacy.' It's like when you talk nicely to your friends to sort out a disagreement!

Hoping for a Happy Ending

So, my friends, this is a story about a country trying its best to look after its people. Nigeria is working hard to make sure its citizens who left things behind in South Africa get a fair chance to get some help. It shows how important it is for countries to be good neighbors and help each other. We hope that with all this hard work, our Nigerian friends will get the compensation they deserve, and everyone can continue to live happily and safely in our beautiful Africa! Stay tuned for more news from your kid-friendly reporter!
summary: Nigeria is making a list of homes and shops that its citizens left behind in South Africa when they felt unsafe, and is asking for help or money (compensation) to make up for their losses.
whyMatters: This is important because it shows that countries care about their people and will try to help them when they lose important things, making sure everyone is treated fairly and can feel safe.

originalTitle: Sudan's RSF committed crimes against humanity in el-Fasher, Amnesty says
title: Sudan's RSF committed crimes against humanity in el-Fasher, Amnesty says
content: The Amnesty report adds to evidence of atrocities in el-Fasher, which the UN says bore the hallmarks of genocide.

title: Iran football team welcomed home after World Cup exit

originalTitle: E Jean Carroll demands Donald Trump pay $5.8m in damages from 2019 case
title: A Lady Asks a Famous Man for Her Money, and the Court Agrees!
content:
Hello, my wonderful friends all across Africa! It's me, your kid-friendly news reporter, here to share some exciting (and easy-to-understand!) news from far away in America. Today, we're talking about a lady named Ms. E. Jean Carroll and a very famous man, Mr. Donald Trump. They had a big problem, and now it's time for one part of that problem to be sorted out! It's a story about kindness, fairness, and following the rules.

The Big Problem Started a While Ago

Imagine you're playing with your friends, and someone says something not very nice about you. It makes you feel sad, or maybe even a little angry. Well, something like that happened to Ms. E. Jean Carroll a long time ago. A few years back, Mr. Donald Trump, who used to be the President of America, said some things about Ms. Carroll that were not true and made her feel very bad. It hurt her feelings and her good name. In grown-up language, when someone says bad things that are not true and it hurts you, it's a bit like breaking a rule of kindness. Ms. Carroll felt that Mr. Trump broke that rule, and it upset her very much. She felt that what he said hurt how people saw her. So, what do grown-ups do when they have a big problem like this, especially when it involves unfair words? They go to a special place called a court! It's where big problems get solved with rules and fairness.

What is a Court, Anyway?

Did you know? A court is like a big, important playground for grown-ups! But instead of playing games, they solve problems and make sure everyone follows the rules. It’s where people go to make sure things are fair. In court, there are special helpers called judges. Judges are like super-wise teachers who listen to everyone's story. They know all the rules and decide what is fair. They help to make sure that everyone gets a chance to speak. Ms. Carroll decided to ask the court for help. She wanted the judge to listen to her story and decide if Mr. Trump had done something wrong by saying those unkind things. She wanted someone to say that it wasn't right. She also had special helpers called lawyers. Lawyers are like super-smart detectives who help people tell their story clearly in court. They know all the court rules and how to ask questions to help their clients. They are very important in court.

The Judge Made a Big Decision!

So, everyone went to court. Ms. Carroll, with her lawyers, told her side of the story. Mr. Trump also had his lawyers who shared his side. They all spoke to the judge and explained everything. The judge listened very carefully to all the words and all the reasons. After hearing everything, the judge made a big decision. The judge said that Mr. Trump *did* say things that were not true about Ms. Carroll, and it was not fair. The judge agreed with Ms. Carroll. Because Mr. Trump's words had hurt Ms. Carroll so much, the judge said he needed to pay her some money. This special money is called "damages." It's a way to try and fix some of the hurt. Did you know? "Damages" is a grown-up word for money given to someone to help them feel a little better after they've been hurt, especially when their feelings or good name were hurt! It's like saying, "We are sorry this happened, and this money is to help make things a little bit right again." It doesn't make the bad thing disappear, but it helps.

Asking for a Second Look

Now, when a judge makes a decision, sometimes one of the people involved might not agree. They might say, "Please, can another, even more important judge look at this?" This is called an "appeal." It means asking for a second check on the decision. Mr. Donald Trump did not agree with the first decision. He asked another court, a higher court with more judges, to look at the case again. He hoped that a new group of judges would say something different, and that he wouldn't have to pay the money. So, the case went to a different court, a higher court. But those judges also listened and looked at everything very carefully. And guess what? They agreed with the first judge! They said, "Yes, the first decision was fair and correct." So, two courts had now agreed.

The Super-Important Court Says "No More!"

But Mr. Trump still wanted to try one more time! He asked the *highest* court in America, called the US Supreme Court, to look at his case again. This court is the biggest and most powerful court of all. Did you know? The US Supreme Court is like the head teacher of *all* the courts in America! When they make a decision, it's usually the final, final word. There's no one else higher to ask after them. Their word is very important. So, Mr. Trump asked the Supreme Court. The Supreme Court judges looked at his request. And just recently, they said, "No." They said, "We have looked, and we agree with the other courts. There's no need for us to look at this case again." This means they won't change the decision. This was a very important "no"! It means the decision made by the first judge and the other courts stands. It means Mr. Trump still has to pay the money. All the courts have now agreed on what is fair.

Time to Get the Money!

Now that the highest court has said "no more looking," Ms. Carroll's helpers (her lawyers) have a very clear message. They are saying, "Okay, the talking is over. The courts have all agreed. Now it's time for Mr. Trump to pay the money he was told to pay!" They want this to happen quickly. They want a "fast solution" to getting the money. It's like when you finish all your chores, you want your pocket money right away, not next month! They don't want to wait any longer. The amount of money is very, very big: $5.8 million! That's a huge pile of money. It's an amount that is hard to imagine for most people. Did you know? $5.8 million is enough money to buy thousands and thousands of toys! Or build many, many schools and hospitals for children. It's a huge amount that can do so many things!

Why Is This Story Important for You?

This story might seem like it's just about grown-ups and money, but it has a very important lesson for everyone, even for children like you! It teaches us about fairness and speaking truthfully. It shows us that rules are important, and everyone must follow them. Even very famous and powerful people like Mr. Donald Trump must follow the rules. No one is above the law, no matter how important they are. When someone says unkind or untrue things, especially if it hurts another person, there can be consequences. Consequences mean what happens after you do something. Good choices have good consequences, and bad choices can have not-so-good consequences. It also shows that if you feel someone has treated you unfairly or said bad things about you, there are ways to ask for help, like going to a court. You can find people who will listen and help make things right. This story tells us that being fair and honest is very important, and that justice (making things right) can happen, even if it takes a little bit of time. It's a reminder that everyone is equal under the rules, and that our words have power, so we must use them kindly. So, my amazing friends, that's the big news from far away today! A lady asked for her money because of unkind words, and after many courts looked, they all agreed she should get it. It's a good reminder to always be kind and truthful with your words. That's all for now from your kid-friendly news reporter here in Africa! Keep smiling and keep being kind! Bye-bye!
summary: A lady named Ms. E. Jean Carroll will finally get a large sum of money from Mr. Donald Trump after the highest court in America agreed he must pay for saying unkind words about her.
whyMatters: This story is important because it shows that even very famous and powerful people must follow the rules, and that saying unkind things that are not true can have serious consequences.

originalTitle: Genuine superstar Kane produces his biggest England moment
title: Genuine superstar Kane produces his biggest England moment
content: Harry Kane's England career is filled with great moments - his World Cup rescue act against DR Congo in Atlanta is his greatest, says chief football writer Phil McNulty.

originalTitle: Kane scores twice as England come from behind to reach last 16
title: Kane scores twice as England come from behind to reach last 16
content: Harry Kane scores twice as England come from behind to beat DR Congo in a tense World Cup encounter in Atlanta, and set up a last-16 meeting with Mexico.

originalTitle: No time for England to adapt to altitude - Tuchel
title: No time for England to adapt to altitude - Tuchel
content: Mexico will have a "huge advantage" against England in the World Cup last-16 tie because of the altitude of the Azteca Stadium in Mexico City, says head coach Thomas Tuchel.
